Being accused of theft in Burbank, California, is a serious matter that requires an experienced lawyer to defend your rights and protect your future.

Theft crimes are taken very seriously, and a conviction could result in severe penalties such as jail time, fines, and a permanent criminal record.

At Farris Law Firm, we are dedicated to helping alleged theft offenders navigate the complex legal system and achieve the best possible outcome for their case.

Contact a Burbank theft crimes lawyer online or at (818) 861-0861 to discuss how we can help you build a strong defense against theft charges.

Why Choose a Burbank Theft Crimes Lawyer from Farris Law?

As a private criminal defense law firm, our legal professionals can provide personal attention, customized strategies, and aggressive representation for individuals facing theft charges in Burbank.

We have a strong track record of success in proving innocence, reducing charges, or having cases dismissed completely.

Whether you have been charged with a misdemeanor or felony theft crime in Burbank, our team has the experience to fight for your rights and protect your future.

Our convenient office location makes it easy to come in and discuss your case with compassionate and understanding attorneys.

Unable to make it for an in-personal case evaluation? No problem. Our legal professionals can provide comprehensive legal support where and how it suits you best.

What Theft Crimes in Burbank Can Farris Law Help Me With?

Theft is a broad legal term that encompasses many different types of crimes, including but not limited to:

  • Petty Theft: The taking of property valued at $950 or less without the owner’s permission. This is considered a misdemeanor offense in California.
  • Grand Theft: The taking of property valued at more than $950 without the owner’s permission. This may be charged as either a misdemeanor or felony, depending on the circumstances.
  • Burglary: The unlawful entry into a structure with the intent to commit theft or any other felony. This is usually charged as a felony offense.
  • Robbery: Taking of property from another person against their will through force or fear. This is considered a serious felony offense in California.

No matter what type of theft crime you are facing, our experienced attorneys have the experience to build strong defense strategies that challenge the prosecution’s narrative and work towards achieving the best possible outcome.

What Strategies Does a Burbank Theft Crimes Lawyer Use?

An experienced Burbank theft crimes attorney from Farris Law Firm will utilize a variety of strategies to defend against theft charges, including but not limited to:

  • Challenging the evidence: Your lawyer may challenge the validity or admissibility of evidence presented by the prosecution, such as witness testimonies or physical evidence.
  • Claim of right: Your lawyer may argue that you had a legal right to the property in question and therefore did not commit a theft.
  • Proving lack of intent: To convict you of a theft crime, the prosecution must prove that you had criminal intent. Your lawyer may work to show that you did not intend to commit a crime.
  • Negotiating with prosecutors: Your lawyer may negotiate with prosecutors for reduced charges or penalties in exchange for a guilty plea.
  • Presenting an alibi: If you have a strong alibi, your lawyer may use this to prove that you were not present at the time of the alleged theft.

You should never assume that the charges against you are set in stone. An experienced Burbank theft defense lawyer will work tirelessly to fight for your rights and explore all possible defense strategies to achieve a favorable outcome.

What Are the Penalties for Theft in Burbank?

Without an experienced defense lawyer in Burbank, you may face the most extreme penalties for theft crimes, including but not limited to:

  • Jail time: Depending on the severity of the offense and your criminal history, you may face jail time ranging from a few days to several years.
  • Fines: Theft crimes can result in significant fines, usually based on the value of the stolen property.
  • Restitution: A guilty verdict may require you to pay restitution for damages and losses the victim(s) sustained due to the theft.
  • Probation: The court may sentence you to probation with conditions such as community service or counseling.

In addition to these penalties, a conviction for theft can also have long-term consequences, such as difficulty finding employment, obtaining loans, or renting housing.

Don’t let a theft accusation in Burbank jeopardize your future. Trust our experienced lawyers at Farris Law Firm to fight for your rights and protect your reputation.

Burbank Theft Crimes Attorney FAQs

Can I get my theft charges expunged in Burbank?

If the court convicts you of a theft crime in Burbank, you may expunge the conviction after completing your sentence and probation. However, not all convictions qualify, and you will want our attorneys to guide you through this complex process.

Will I go to jail for a first-time theft offense in Burbank?

The penalties for a first-time theft offense will depend on the circumstances of your case. It is possible to avoid jail time with the help of an experienced defense lawyer who may argue for alternative sentencing options.

How do I choose the right attorney for my Burbank theft case?

When choosing an attorney, you must look for one with experience, a track record of success, and the ability to provide personalized attention. At Farris Law Firm, we have a team of experienced attorneys who are dedicated to fighting for our clients’ rights and achieving the best possible outcome for their case.

Will I go to jail for a misdemeanor theft charge in Burbank?

It is possible to face jail time for a misdemeanor theft charge in Burbank, depending on the specific circumstances of your case. However, with the help of an experienced attorney, you may negotiate reduced charges or penalties that do not involve jail time.

I have been falsely accused of theft. What should I do?

If you have been falsely accused of theft in Burbank, seek legal help immediately. Our attorneys can help combat the prosecution’s narrative and work towards a favorable outcome in your case.

Trust a Burbank Theft Crimes Lawyer From Farris Law Firm To Protect Your Rights
